{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks about the staging of angiofibroma when it extends to one or more paranasal sinuses. Reviewing the provided documents, several staging systems for nasopharyngeal angiofibroma are described. Document [1] states: 'Stage I disease is confined to the nasal cavity and nasopharynx. Stage II involves the paranasal sinuses and lateral pterygopalatine fossa without residual vascularity.' This directly indicates that extension into the paranasal sinuses defines Stage II. Document [2] further supports this: 'Stage Ib is defined by extension into the paranasal sinuses.' Document [3] also confirms: 'Stage II includes pterygopalatine fossa or any sinus invasion.' Therefore, when angiofibroma extends to one or more paranasal sinuses, it is classified as Stage II. The other stages (III, IV) are associated with skull base erosion, intracranial extension, or involvement of the orbit and infratemporal fossa, which are more advanced and not described in the context of sinus extension alone. Thus, the correct stage for extension into paranasal sinuses is Stage II.", "answer_choice": "B"}
